summary_one_line: Input must encode a parseable pop3-style URL header so curl reaches the poll/socket-check path with an upstream socket pending.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured fuzz-input whose protocol-scheme bytes spell 'pop3' at offsets 0x06-0x09 and whose subsequent bytes form a host:port URL (e.g. '127.0.0.1:9001') plus an HTTP-style request line ('GHTTP/' at 0x24-0x29) — i.e. the input matches curl's expected protocol/URL string CMPs so the fuzzer harness drives curl into the upload/connect path where Curl_socket_check is invoked with a writefd socket whose revents come back WITHOUT (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP), taking the false side of the readfd0 revents mask test.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed contains the literal ASCII 'pop3' (70 6f 70 33) at offsets 0x06-0x09 and a CRLF-terminated URL+HTTP-header payload (`127.0.0.1:9001`, `GHTTP/`, `mewhere\r\n`). HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE confirms the winners enter the upload path (Curl_get_upload_buffer 20 hits, ossl_seed/ossl_random 20 hits, Curl_fillreadbuffer 10, Curl_done_sending 10, readwrite_upload 10) — all zero for the loser. At branch L231 the winner takes both T (14) and F (10); the loser only ever takes T (10) and never F.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are 32-38 bytes of garbled bytes with no 'pop3' magic at 0x06-0x09 (offsets show 1Z`Gee^, p3:0/12, A22%e8, +-k7%E, %AAA — random havoc). They never satisfy curl's scheme strcmp, so the harness never wires up the upload path; Curl_socket_check is only ever called on a readfd path where revents arrives with POLLIN/POLLRDNORM set (true side), never on the writefd path where the false side becomes reachable.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace observes the strcmp/memcmp CMP_MAP entries when curl parses the scheme ('pop3', 'http://', host, port) and substitutes those literal operands into the input at the matching offsets. Naive lacks CMP interception entirely (per MECHANISM CONTEXT, 'No I2S'), so it must brute-force every byte of the protocol header by havoc, which is statistically infeasible inside the 12h budget — naive blocks 10/10 trials while cmplog resolves 10/10 within ~66-3064s. The lineage of two winning seeds (1abae053b6f68107, 06c4f49e154580bb) both terminate in I2S-floor dash rows (depth 7-8 and 16-17 respectively), directly confirming I2SRandReplace contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is at curl/lib/select.c:231 in Curl_socket_check, false side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Location and Globally blocked side lines
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Location: /src/curl/lib/select.c:231:8
Enclosing function: Curl_socket_check
Source line:     if(pfd[num].revents & (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP))
Globally blocked side: F

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolves 10/10 while naive blocks 10/10 with the largest possible prob_div=1.0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is the single canonical I2S delta (cmplog vs naive).
Every winner-resolving seed contains the literal ASCII 'pop3' bytes at offsets 0x06-0x09.
Winning seeds embed a host:port URL fragment '127.0.0.1:9001'.
Winning seeds embed an HTTP-style request line and CRLF-terminated header.
BYTE DIFF flags offsets 0x06-0x09 as a strong magic-bytes divergence: all 10 winners hold 70/6f/70 and 6f/33; loser bytes are 10+ distinct values.
Loser-blocking seeds are short (32-38 bytes) garbled inputs that do not contain the pop3 scheme.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Seed 1 (id=0022f5056b29d1a1, size=32 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=73s, mutation_op=BytesInsertMutator,BytesDeleteMutator,QwordAddMutator,QwordAddMutator):
  0000: 00 01 00 00 00 19 31 5a 60 47 65 65 5e 25 5a 5a

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the winner takes both T and F directions while the loser only ever takes T and never F.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 231  T=14 F=10  T=10 F=0  if(pfd[num].revents & (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner exclusively enters the upload/TLS path (Curl_get_upload_buffer, ossl_seed, ossl_random, readwrite_upload, etc.) while the loser hits zero of those — proof the loser never advances past curl's protocol-scheme parsing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
20         0  Curl_get_upload_buffer  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:118-125)
      20         0  openssl.c:ossl_seed  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:807-883)
      20         0  openssl.c:ossl_random  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:4438-4451)
      16         0  Curl_single_getsock  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:1352-1387)
      16         0  openssl.c:rand_enough  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:802-804)
      10         0  Curl_fillreadbuffer  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:163-363)
      10         0  Curl_done_sending  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:882-896)
      10         0  transfer.c:readwrite_upload  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:928-1154)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mutator set includes I2SRandReplace, which scans input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S — by mechanism it cannot perform operand substitution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) in cmplog/vpc lineage are direct lower-bound evidence of I2SRandReplace finds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
silent ParentInfo-only entries
(`mutation_op = -` in lineage output) that — in cmplog/vpc only —
indicate an I2SRandReplace find under the current build. The dash
rows are exclusive to cmplog and value_profile_cmplog; their
presence in a winning seed's ancestor chain is direct (lower-bound)
evidence of I2S contribution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 313 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 1abae053b6f68107` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 7 and 8 of the 9-level ancestor chain; cross-checked seed 06c4f49e154580bb (the TokenReplace-tagged leaf at 2355s) which converges on the same I2S-floor ancestors 92cece3b9f534109 and 1ce58f232eec81f2 at depths 16 and 17. Both winning lineages share the same I2S ancestor pair, confirming I2SRandReplace produced the foundational protocol-scheme substitution that made the upload path reachable.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only winning constraint is a 4-byte literal 'pop3' equality CMP at a fixed input offset should show cmplog>>naive at scan_value=4 bytes; if naive resolves it within the same budget (e.g. <=2x cmplog's median time-to-resolve) then the 4-byte magic-string gate is NOT what blocks naive here and the mechanism would be refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly read the curl fuzz harness source to confirm the 'pop3'+URL+HTTP-line layout is exactly what it parses; the input-feature description is inferred from BYTE DIFF concentration and HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE rather than from the harness code.
